mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0% over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of industrial production and housing starts signals synchronized real-economy demand expansion. This dual surge validates earnings growth forecasts, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO change × HOUST change) shows no predictive power for 20-day SPX returns (p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation combined with an accelerating federal funds rate forces equity risk premiums higher, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 60% of observations where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS show concurrent month-over-month increases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent annualized rate confirms robust aggregate demand, directly supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where real GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annualized over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid expansion in retail sales indicates strong consumer confidence and spending momentum, which translates directly into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant difference between periods of rapid retail sales growth and periods of stagnation (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms structural labor market strength. This dual confirmation reduces recession probabilities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 60-day SPX returns across three consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production outpaces inflation, real supply capacity expands faster than demand, easing cost pressures and supporting corporate margins. This dynamic generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change exceeds CPIAUCSL month-over-month change
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth consistently outpaces CPIAUCSL growth for three consecutive months over a 5-year test window.
